user.title: Onna — setup
user.description: Enter the connection details for your Onna device. The integration will connect automatically to discover all configured zones and entities.

data_description.host: Local IP address of the Onna device on your network (e.g. 192.168.1.10). You can find it in your router or in the Onna app under Settings → Device info.
data_description.onna_id: Unique device identifier. Find it in the Onna app under Settings → Device info (field 'ID').

error.invalid_host: Enter a valid IP address or hostname (letters, digits, dots and hyphens only).
error.invalid_onna_id: Enter a valid Onna ID (letters, digits, hyphens and underscores only).
error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to the device. Check that the IP address is correct and that the Onna is powered on and reachable from this server.
error.no_devices_found: The device responded but returned no configured climate zones. Check the configuration in the Onna app.

zone_picker.title: Onna — configure zone
zone_picker.description: Select the climate zone you want to customise. You can add an external temperature sensor (more accurate than the thermostat's built-in probe) and a window sensor to automatically turn off the zone when the window has been open for more than 10 minutes. When you are done with one zone, go back to Configure to adjust the others.

data_description.zone: Choose the zone you want to configure. You can repeat the process for each zone independently.

zone_detail.title: Onna — external sensors for zone
zone_detail.description: Both fields are optional. Leave a field blank to disable that feature for this zone and use the default behaviour (built-in thermostat probe / no window detection).

data_description.temp_sensor: A HA sensor entity (sensor domain) measuring the actual room temperature in °C. Once set, the temperature displayed in HA and the setpoint sent to the KNX bus are automatically corrected to compensate for the difference between the thermostat's built-in probe and the real room temperature. If this sensor becomes unavailable, the integration falls back to the built-in probe without interruption.
data_description.window_sensor: A HA binary sensor entity (binary_sensor domain) linked to a window or door contact sensor. When the sensor reports 'on' (open) for more than 10 minutes, the zone turns off automatically to save energy. The zone resumes as soon as the closure is detected. Zones that were already manually turned off are not affected.

general.title: Onna — general settings
general.description: Installation-wide tuning. These values apply to all climate zones.

data_description.setpoint_hysteresis: Minimum change in the compensated setpoint before a new value is written to the KNX bus. Lower values track the external sensor more closely but generate more bus traffic; higher values reduce traffic at the cost of precision. Set 0.0 to write every change. Default: 0.5 °C.
data_description.window_open_delay: How long a window must stay open before its zone thermostat is paused. Short ventilation bursts below this time are ignored. Default: 600 seconds (10 minutes).
